items.title: Path construction
items.prefer: Path objects joined with the / operator.
items.example: `Path('data') / 'docs' / f'{doc_id}.txt'` — clearer than `os.path.join(...)`, and correct on every OS.

items.title: Secrets and config
items.avoid: Hard-coding API keys or absolute machine paths.
items.prefer: Read from os.environ with sensible defaults and fail fast if required ones are missing.
items.why: Keeps secrets out of version control and makes deployments portable.
items.example:
```python
DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get('DATA_DIR', './data'))
API_KEY  = os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY']   # required → KeyError at startup
```

items.title: Directory creation
items.why: Avoids FileNotFoundError and race-prone existence checks.
items.example: `out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` right before `out.write_text(...)` — idempotent, safe under concurrency.

items.title: Encoding
items.avoid: Relying on the platform default text encoding.
items.why: Guarantees consistent behavior across machines and OSes.
items.example: `Path('doc.md').read_text(encoding='utf-8')` works the same on Linux (utf-8 default) and Windows (cp1252 default) — same text, no mojibake.
